name: Historical Museum
description: The Tagum City Historical and Cultural Center is a cultural landmark that showcases the rich history and heritage of Tagum through artifacts, exhibits, and stories of its people. It offers visitors a glimpse into the city’s past and cultural identity in a modern and engaging setting.
location: Rizal St, Tagum, Davao del Norte
accessibility: The center is easily accessible within Tagum City via tricycles, jeepneys, and taxis, with nearby major roads and transport routes. It is also wheelchair-accessible, making it inclusive for all visitors.
features:
Kagikan Museum (heritage exhibits of Tagum)
Tagumpay Theater (large performance venue)
City Library and Learning Commons
Sentro Rizal Gallery
facilities:
Modern exhibition spaces
Theater and event halls
Library and study areas
Open cultural spaces for programs and activities
best_time: Located along Osmeña Street in Tagum City, it is about 1–2 hours from Davao City by bus or private vehicle. Local transport is readily available within the city
what_to_pack:
Comfortable clothes and walking shoes
Water bottle
Camera or phone for photos
Light bag for personal items
visiting_rules:
Follow museum etiquette (no touching exhibits unless allowed)
Maintain cleanliness and proper behavior
Observe silence in exhibit and learning areas
Follow staff instructions and posted rules

name: Energy Park (Epark)
description: The Energy Park is a spacious and vibrant recreational park known for its lush greenery, open spaces, and family-friendly atmosphere. It offers a relaxing environment where visitors can enjoy outdoor activities such as jogging, biking, picnicking, and leisure walks.
location: CR8F+7VW, Unnamed Road, Tagum, Davao del Norte
accessibility: The Energy Park is located in Barangay Apokon and is easily accessible by tricycles, jeepneys, and private vehicles. It is near main roads and can be reached within minutes from the city center.
features:
Wide open green spaces for relaxation and recreation
Jogging and biking trails
Children’s playground and picnic areas
Large fountain and event stage
Mini forest park and camping areas
facilities:
Children Park
Bike rental services
Food stalls and refreshment vendors
Restrooms and seating areas (benches, gazebos)
Government offices and training centers within the park
best_time: The park is about 1–2 hours from Davao City by bus or private vehicle. It is open daily (around 5:00 AM to 10:00 PM) and has no entrance fee, making it a popular destination for locals and tourists.
what_to_pack:
Comfortable clothes and rubber shoes
Water bottle
Picnic mat or blanket
Sunscreen or cap for daytime visits
Camera or phone for photos
visiting_rules:
Keep the park clean and dispose of trash properly
Respect nature and avoid damaging plants or facilities
Follow designated areas for biking, jogging, and picnics
Supervise children in play areas
Follow park regulations and staff instructions

name: Nabintad River Side Park
description: The Nabintad Riverside is a peaceful nature spot known for its flowing river, cool surroundings, and scenic greenery. It offers a relaxing escape where visitors can enjoy the calm ambiance, connect with nature, and experience local culture.
accessibility: Located within Tagum City, Nabintad Riverside can be reached by tricycles, motorcycles, and private vehicles. Some areas may require a short walk, especially when accessing riverside spots.
features:
Natural river with clear flowing water
Shaded areas surrounded by trees
Cultural presence of local communities
Picnic and relaxation spots
Scenic views perfect for nature trips
facilities:
Basic cottages and seating areas
Open spaces for gatherings
Limited small stores or local vendors
Simple rest areas
best_time: The site is a short drive from Tagum City proper. It is best visited during dry weather for safer and more enjoyable river activities. Entrance fees may be minimal or community-based.
what_to_pack:
Extra clothes and towel
Slippers or water-friendly footwear
Food and drinking water
Trash bag for clean-up
Sunscreen or insect repellent
visiting_rules:
Keep the area clean and avoid littering
Respect local communities and their culture
Avoid disturbing the natural environment
Be cautious when swimming in the river
Follow any guidelines set by locals or caretakers
